figures.caption: Table 16: Key hyperparameter settings of ARG-Designer.
Table 7: Task-oriented role families and summarized tool categories in the agent pool.

Figure 1: Current Multi-agent Systems (MAS) show inflexibility in different aspects, which have fixed topologies like (a) and (c), or round-wise repetitions like (a) and (b). Though (c) Mixtureof-Agents (MoA) provides the potential of cross-agent interactions in steps, it still follows static structures with fixed numbers of agents and layers. Intuitively, we expect (d) adaptive MoA, which can flexibly determine any number of agents in each step to implicitly simulate arbitrary communication topologies, and adaptively extend with steps, yielding spatio-temporal flexibility.

Table 2: Accuracy comparison with three types of baselines, including Single-Agent Systems, Multi-Agent Systems, Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Systems. All baselines and DMoA are driven by gpt-oss-120b (Vanilla). Red: the best, Blue: the runner-up.

Table 8: Scalability of DMoA under different agent configurations on MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval, and SVAMP. The configuration (N,K) denotes using N candidate agents and selecting K agents at each reasoning step. As the configuration size increases, DMoA consistently improves accuracy across all benchmarks, while the marginal gain gradually saturates. Token consumption is estimated from the corresponding efficiency curves and follows an approximately linear scaling trend.
Table 11: Key hyperparameter settings of G-Designer.
Table 10: Key hyperparameter settings of GPTSwarm.
Table 12: Key hyperparameter settings of AFlow.

Figure 7: Robustness analysis under different adversarial-agent ratios. We compare the average accuracy of different multi-agent systems before and after prompt-based attacks, where a certain proportion of normal agents is replaced by adversarial agents. Results are reported under 20%, 40%, 60%, and 80% attacked-agent settings.
Table 13: Key hyperparameter settings of SpecReason.
Table 15: Key hyperparameter settings of SafeSieve.
Table 14: Key hyperparameter settings of STEER.
Figure 5: Visualization of accuracy and consumption of multi-agent systems across MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, and SVAMP. The diameters of circles represent the scales of token consumption.

Figure 2: The overview of DMoA. An agent pool is initialized to possess diverse expert capabilities. During optimization, DMoA runs all agents to collect the predictive entropy, and utilize it as the supervision signal. During inference, only several agents are activated in each reasoning step.
